critic_raw:
ASSESSOR 1:
Holiday auto-decline policy, seasonal shift, and no controlled backtest. The chargeback reduction is not attributable to the model. Verdict: critique_wins.
defender_raw:
ASSESSOR 2:
Model architectural improvements are genuine. But holiday auto-decline prevents chargebacks from high-loss categories without any model involvement. The seasonal comparison adds additional confounding. Verdict: critique_wins.
adjudication_raw:
ASSESSOR 3:
All three must-find issues identified by both assessors.

SYNTHESIS:
Uniform critique_wins. The production result conflates model quality with policy effects and seasonal fraud mix changes.

ETD SPECIFICATION:
- Measure: On a non-holiday traffic sample, score all transactions with both old and new models under identical review/decline rules; compare precision@top-N and chargeback capture rates
- Success criterion: New model shows no meaningful improvement on matched traffic — confirming production savings were policy-driven
- Failure criterion: New model shows materially higher chargeback capture at the same review rate on matched traffic — confirming genuine model improvement

empirical_test.measure: Precision and chargeback capture rates on matched non-holiday traffic under identical rules
empirical_test.success_criterion: No meaningful improvement — policy explains production savings
empirical_test.failure_criterion: Materially higher chargeback capture at matched review rate — genuine model improvement
